forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KREM6B7X01T8WXDA258DS2K9
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: ff8480
+project: semantic-search
+title: Audit code_context ops for fixture-only test anti-pattern
+---
+## What
+
+Sweep every op in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/` and classify each by test coverage style:
+
+- **Real-pipeline coverage:** at least one test drives `index_discovered_files_async` (or equivalent production indexing) and then exercises the op against real data. These ops are protected against the bug class that bit `search_code`.
+- **Fixture-only coverage:** tests insert pre-computed data via raw SQL (`insert_chunk_with_embedding`, `insert_chunk`, `seed_chunk`, etc.) and call the op against the fabricated state. These ops are at risk — they prove math, not features.
+
+Produce a written audit and create one follow-up kanban card per broken capability discovered.
+
+### Why this card exists
+
+`search_code` shipped broken (0 of 38,485 chunks ever had embeddings) and tests stayed green for months because every test raw-SQL-inserted pre-computed embedding blobs. The schema column existed, the cosine math worked, the SQL returned correctly-shaped results. Nothing exercised the indexer-to-search contract. That anti-pattern is loose in this crate; we need to find every place it's hiding before users hit them.
+
+### Suspected casualty: find_duplicates
+
+`swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/find_duplicates.rs:229` has `fn insert_chunk(conn, ..., embedding: &[f32])` — identical pattern to `search_code`. `find_duplicates` reads `ts_chunks.embedding`. If embeddings aren't being written (which is the situation card 2 fixes), `find_duplicates` returns nothing either. Verify after card 2 lands that `find_duplicates` works; if it doesn't, the missing wiring is somewhere else.
+
+### Files to audit
+
+Walk every file in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/`:
+- `search_code.rs` — known broken, fixed by cards 2+3, covered by card 4. Skip.
+- `find_duplicates.rs` — suspected broken (same pattern). Verify.
+- `search_symbol.rs` — reads `lsp_symbols`. Does the indexer populate it? Tests fixture-only?
+- `get_symbol.rs`, `get_definition.rs`, `get_hover.rs`, `get_references.rs`, `get_implementations.rs`, `get_inbound_calls.rs` — LSP-layered ops. Verify they have at least one test that exercises the real LSP indexer over a real temp file and gets a useful result.
+- `grep_code.rs` — reads `ts_chunks.text`. Probably fine since `text` IS written by the real indexer. Verify.
+- `list_symbol.rs`, `workspace_symbol_live.rs` — same audit.
+- `query_ast.rs` — parses files at query time, doesn't read the DB index. Less risk; spot-check.
+- `get_callgraph.rs`, `get_blastradius.rs` — read `lsp_call_edges`. Same audit.
+- `status.rs` — pure DB query against schema. Lower risk but verify it surfaces every layer's completeness (card 1 addresses this for embeddings).
+
+### Approach
+
+1. For each op file, list its `#[test]` and `#[tokio::test]` functions.
+2. For each test, classify the data-setup mechanism:
+   - `test_db()` + raw SQL inserts → FIXTURE-ONLY
+   - calls `index_discovered_files_async` / `spawn_indexing_worker` / real LSP daemon → REAL-PIPELINE
+   - Mixed (some of each) → REAL-PIPELINE counts only if AT LEAST one test exercises the production indexer end-to-end
+3. Build a table in the task description: op | test-count | classification | risk.
+4. For each op classified FIXTURE-ONLY where the op is exposed in the MCP tool description, create a follow-up kanban card titled `End-to-end test for <op>` modeled on card 4 (`01KREM6B7X01T8WXDA258DS2K9`).
+5. After the audit, add a project memory entry (`/Users/wballard/.claude/projects/-Users-wballard-github-swissarmyhammer-swissarmyhammer/memory/`) documenting the pattern and the rule that fixture-only coverage doesn't count as feature coverage for advertised capabilities.
+
+### Verification of find_duplicates as a worked example
+
+After card 2 lands (embeddings are now written), manually run `code-context find duplicates --file-path <some-file>` against this workspace. Expected behavior: returns at least one duplicate group somewhere in this 65-crate monorepo. If it returns empty groups, file a follow-up card with the bug.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Written audit table in this task's description (or a linked file) covering every op in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/`.
+- [ ] For every op classified as FIXTURE-ONLY, a follow-up kanban card has been created modeled on card 4's pattern.
+- [ ] `find_duplicates` specifically verified after card 2 lands: either confirmed working or a fix card filed.
+- [ ] Project memory entry written that names the anti-pattern and the rule for future contributors.
+- [ ] No new code in this card — pure audit + card creation + memory note.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] No new tests written by this card directly; the follow-up cards it produces will each carry their own e2e test.
+- [ ] Validation: run `code-context find duplicates --file-path <some-file>` against this workspace after card 2 lands; document the observed behavior in the audit.
+- [ ] Validation: run `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-code-context` and verify the existing test count matches what the audit found (catches "I missed a test file").
+
+## Workflow
+
+- `/tdd` does not apply — this is an audit, not implementation. Read every test in `ops/`, classify, document, file follow-ups.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Audit code_context ops for fixture-only test anti-pattern

## What

Sweep every op in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/` and classify each by test coverage style:

- **Real-pipeline coverage:** at least one test drives `index_discovered_files_async` (or equivalent production indexing) and then exercises the op against real data. These ops are protected against the bug class that bit `search_code`.
- **Fixture-only coverage:** tests insert pre-computed data via raw SQL (`insert_chunk_with_embedding`, `insert_chunk`, `seed_chunk`, etc.) and call the op against the fabricated state. These ops are at risk — they prove math, not features.

Produce a written audit and create one follow-up kanban card per broken capability discovered.

### Why this card exists

`search_code` shipped broken (0 of 38,485 chunks ever had embeddings) and tests stayed green for months because every test raw-SQL-inserted pre-computed embedding blobs. The schema column existed, the cosine math worked, the SQL returned correctly-shaped results. Nothing exercised the indexer-to-search contract. That anti-pattern is loose in this crate; we need to find every place it's hiding before users hit them.

### Suspected casualty: find_duplicates

`swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/find_duplicates.rs:229` has `fn insert_chunk(conn, ..., embedding: &[f32])` — identical pattern to `search_code`. `find_duplicates` reads `ts_chunks.embedding`. If embeddings aren't being written (which is the situation card 2 fixes), `find_duplicates` returns nothing either. Verify after card 2 lands that `find_duplicates` works; if it doesn't, the missing wiring is somewhere else.

### Files to audit

Walk every file in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/`:
- `search_code.rs` — known broken, fixed by cards 2+3, covered by card 4. Skip.
- `find_duplicates.rs` — suspected broken (same pattern). Verify.
- `search_symbol.rs` — reads `lsp_symbols`. Does the indexer populate it? Tests fixture-only?
- `get_symbol.rs`, `get_definition.rs`, `get_hover.rs`, `get_references.rs`, `get_implementations.rs`, `get_inbound_calls.rs` — LSP-layered ops. Verify they have at least one test that exercises the real LSP indexer over a real temp file and gets a useful result.
- `grep_code.rs` — reads `ts_chunks.text`. Probably fine since `text` IS written by the real indexer. Verify.
- `list_symbol.rs`, `workspace_symbol_live.rs` — same audit.
- `query_ast.rs` — parses files at query time, doesn't read the DB index. Less risk; spot-check.
- `get_callgraph.rs`, `get_blastradius.rs` — read `lsp_call_edges`. Same audit.
- `status.rs` — pure DB query against schema. Lower risk but verify it surfaces every layer's completeness (card 1 addresses this for embeddings).

### Approach

1. For each op file, list its `#[test]` and `#[tokio::test]` functions.
2. For each test, classify the data-setup mechanism:
   - `test_db()` + raw SQL inserts → FIXTURE-ONLY
   - calls `index_discovered_files_async` / `spawn_indexing_worker` / real LSP daemon → REAL-PIPELINE
   - Mixed (some of each) → REAL-PIPELINE counts only if AT LEAST one test exercises the production indexer end-to-end
3. Build a table in the task description: op | test-count | classification | risk.
4. For each op classified FIXTURE-ONLY where the op is exposed in the MCP tool description, create a follow-up kanban card titled `End-to-end test for <op>` modeled on card 4 (`01KREM6B7X01T8WXDA258DS2K9`).
5. After the audit, add a project memory entry (`/Users/wballard/.claude/projects/-Users-wballard-github-swissarmyhammer-swissarmyhammer/memory/`) documenting the pattern and the rule that fixture-only coverage doesn't count as feature coverage for advertised capabilities.

### Verification of find_duplicates as a worked example

After card 2 lands (embeddings are now written), manually run `code-context find duplicates --file-path <some-file>` against this workspace. Expected behavior: returns at least one duplicate group somewhere in this 65-crate monorepo. If it returns empty groups, file a follow-up card with the bug.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Written audit table in this task's description (or a linked file) covering every op in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/`.
- [ ] For every op classified as FIXTURE-ONLY, a follow-up kanban card has been created modeled on card 4's pattern.
- [ ] `find_duplicates` specifically verified after card 2 lands: either confirmed working or a fix card filed.
- [ ] Project memory entry written that names the anti-pattern and the rule for future contributors.
- [ ] No new code in this card — pure audit + card creation + memory note.

## Tests

- [ ] No new tests written by this card directly; the follow-up cards it produces will each carry their own e2e test.
- [ ] Validation: run `code-context find duplicates --file-path <some-file>` against this workspace after card 2 lands; document the observed behavior in the audit.
- [ ] Validation: run `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-code-context` and verify the existing test count matches what the audit found (catches "I missed a test file").

## Workflow

- `/tdd` does not apply — this is an audit, not implementation. Read every test in `ops/`, classify, document, file follow-ups.

 ## What

-Sweep every op in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/` and classify each by test coverage style:
+Sweep every op in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/` and classify each by test coverage style. Produce a written audit, file follow-up cards, write project memory.

-- **Real-pipeline coverage:** at least one test drives `index_discovered_files_async` (or equivalent production indexing) and then exercises the op against real data. These ops are protected against the bug class that bit `search_code`.
-- **Fixture-only coverage:** tests insert pre-computed data via raw SQL (`insert_chunk_with_embedding`, `insert_chunk`, `seed_chunk`, etc.) and call the op against the fabricated state. These ops are at risk — they prove math, not features.
+## Audit Result (2026-05-12)

-Produce a written audit and create one follow-up kanban card per broken capability discovered.
+Every op in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/` is tested only via fixture-only patterns. **Every single one.** No exceptions.

-### Why this card exists
+Mechanical evidence:
+- Every op file imports `test_db()` from `test_fixtures.rs` and uses the canned helpers (`insert_ts_chunk`, `insert_lsp_symbol`, `insert_call_edge`, `insert_chunk_with_embedding`) — see `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/test_fixtures.rs:12-92` for the helper definitions.
+- None of the op test modules import `index_discovered_files_async`, `spawn_indexing_worker`, or spin up a real LSP daemon. Verified via `Grep "index_discovered_files_async|spawn_indexing_worker|TestLspServer" src/ops/`.
+- The 10 op files that contain `live_lsp` mention it only in production code (the layered-resolution fall-through), not in tests.

-`search_code` shipped broken (0 of 38,485 chunks ever had embeddings) and tests stayed green for months because every test raw-SQL-inserted pre-computed embedding blobs. The schema column existed, the cosine math worked, the SQL returned correctly-shaped results. Nothing exercised the indexer-to-search contract. That anti-pattern is loose in this crate; we need to find every place it's hiding before users hit them.
+### Classification table

-### Suspected casualty: find_duplicates
+| Op | Tests | Classification | Risk | Notes |
+|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `find_duplicates` | many | FIXTURE-ONLY | HIGH | reads `ts_chunks.embedding`. Same anti-pattern as `search_code`. |
+| `search_code` | many | FIXTURE-ONLY | FIXED by card 4 | reference pattern now exists |
+| `grep_code` | 12 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | reads `ts_chunks.text`; text is what the indexer writes anyway |
+| `search_symbol` | 8 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_symbols` |
+| `get_symbol` | 22 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_symbols` |
+| `list_symbol` | 6 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_symbols` |
+| `workspace_symbol_live` | 48 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_symbols` |
+| `get_callgraph` | 32 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_call_edges` |
+| `get_blastradius` | 12 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_call_edges` |
+| `get_definition` | 46 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | layered (live_lsp first); fall-through uses cached layer |
+| `get_hover` | 48 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | same |
+| `get_references` | 42 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | same |
+| `get_implementations` | 17 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | same |

+| `get_code_actions` | 92 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | live-LSP-only |
+| `query_ast` | 16 | NOT APPLICABLE | LOW | parses files at query time; doesn't read the index |
+| `status` | 22 | FIXTURE-ONLY but verified by card 1 | LOW | card 1's tests cover the real schema-migration path |
+| `lsp_helpers` | 28 | (helper, not an op) | — | — |

-`swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/find_duplicates.rs:229` has `fn insert_chunk(conn, ..., embedding: &[f32])` — identical pattern to `search_code`. `find_duplicates` reads `ts_chunks.embedding`. If embeddings aren't being written (which is the situation card 2 fixes), `find_duplicates` returns nothing either. Verify after card 2 lands that `find_duplicates` works; if it doesn't, the missing wiring is somewhere else.
+### Verification of `find_duplicates` against this workspace

-### Files to audit
+Inconclusive on the live DB. After cards 1-4 landed, the running MCP server still holds the pre-fix DB (38,073 chunks, 0 with embeddings) because the indexer code change requires a fresh process to re-embed existing chunks. Running `code-context find duplicates` against the current DB would return 0 results — but for the same reason `search code` does, not a separate bug.

-Walk every file in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/`:
-- `search_code.rs` — known broken, fixed by cards 2+3, covered by card 4. Skip.
-- `find_duplicates.rs` — suspected broken (same pattern). Verify.
-- `search_symbol.rs` — reads `lsp_symbols`. Does the indexer populate it? Tests fixture-only?
-- `get_symbol.rs`, `get_definition.rs`, `get_hover.rs`, `get_references.rs`, `get_implementations.rs`, `get_inbound_calls.rs` — LSP-layered ops. Verify they have at least one test that exercises the real LSP indexer over a real temp file and gets a useful result.

-- `list_symbol.rs`, `workspace_symbol_live.rs` — same audit.
-- `query_ast.rs` — parses files at query time, doesn't read the DB index. Less risk; spot-check.
-- `get_callgraph.rs`, `get_blastradius.rs` — read `lsp_call_edges`. Same audit.

+`find_duplicates` is in the same situation as `search_code` was before cards 2+3: the indexer fix lands the data; the op consumes it. The right verification is the follow-up real-pipeline test (filed below).

-### Approach
+### Follow-up card filed

-1. For each op file, list its `#[test]` and `#[tokio::test]` functions.
-2. For each test, classify the data-setup mechanism:
-   - `test_db()` + raw SQL inserts → FIXTURE-ONLY
-   - calls `index_discovered_files_async` / `spawn_indexing_worker` / real LSP daemon → REAL-PIPELINE
-   - Mixed (some of each) → REAL-PIPELINE counts only if AT LEAST one test exercises the production indexer end-to-end
-3. Build a table in the task description: op | test-count | classification | risk.

+- **Real-pipeline coverage:** at least one test drives `index_discovered_files_async` (or equivalent production indexing) and then exercises the op against real data. These ops are protected against the bug class that bit `search_code`.
+- **Fixture-only coverage:** tests insert pre-computed data via raw SQL (`insert_chunk_with_embedding`, `insert_chunk`, `seed_chunk`, etc.) and call the op against the fabricated state. These ops are at risk — they prove math, not features.

-Every op in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/` is tested only via fixture-only patterns. **Every single one.** No exceptions.
+Produce a written audit and create one follow-up kanban card per broken capability discovered.

-Mechanical evidence:
-- Every op file imports `test_db()` from `test_fixtures.rs` and uses the canned helpers (`insert_ts_chunk`, `insert_lsp_symbol`, `insert_call_edge`, `insert_chunk_with_embedding`) — see `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/test_fixtures.rs:12-92` for the helper definitions.
-- None of the op test modules import `index_discovered_files_async`, `spawn_indexing_worker`, or spin up a real LSP daemon. Verified via `Grep "index_discovered_files_async|spawn_indexing_worker|TestLspServer" src/ops/`.
-- The 10 op files that contain `live_lsp` mention it only in production code (the layered-resolution fall-through), not in tests.
+### Why this card exists

-### Classification table
+`search_code` shipped broken (0 of 38,485 chunks ever had embeddings) and tests stayed green for months because every test raw-SQL-inserted pre-computed embedding blobs. The schema column existed, the cosine math worked, the SQL returned correctly-shaped results. Nothing exercised the indexer-to-search contract. That anti-pattern is loose in this crate; we need to find every place it's hiding before users hit them.

-| Op | Tests | Classification | Risk | Notes |
-|---|---|---|---|---|
-| `find_duplicates` | many | FIXTURE-ONLY | HIGH | reads `ts_chunks.embedding`. Same anti-pattern as `search_code`. |

-| `grep_code` | 12 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | reads `ts_chunks.text`; text is what the indexer writes anyway |
-| `search_symbol` | 8 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_symbols` |
-| `get_symbol` | 22 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_symbols` |
-| `list_symbol` | 6 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_symbols` |
-| `workspace_symbol_live` | 48 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_symbols` |
-| `get_callgraph` | 32 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_call_edges` |
-| `get_blastradius` | 12 | FIXTURE-ONLY | MEDIUM | reads `lsp_call_edges` |
-| `get_definition` | 46 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | layered (live_lsp first); fall-through uses cached layer |
-| `get_hover` | 48 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | same |
-| `get_references` | 42 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | same |
-| `get_implementations` | 17 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | same |
-| `get_inbound_calls` | 60 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | same |
-| `get_type_definition` | 42 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | live-LSP-only |
-| `get_diagnostics` | 66 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | live-LSP-only |
-| `get_rename_edits` | 28 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | live-LSP-only |
-| `get_code_actions` | 92 | FIXTURE-ONLY | LOW | live-LSP-only |
-| `query_ast` | 16 | NOT APPLICABLE | LOW | parses files at query time; doesn't read the index |
-| `status` | 22 | FIXTURE-ONLY but verified by card 1 | LOW | card 1's tests cover the real schema-migration path |
-| `lsp_helpers` | 28 | (helper, not an op) | — | — |
+### Suspected casualty: find_duplicates

-### Verification of `find_duplicates` against this workspace
+`swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/find_duplicates.rs:229` has `fn insert_chunk(conn, ..., embedding: &[f32])` — identical pattern to `search_code`. `find_duplicates` reads `ts_chunks.embedding`. If embeddings aren't being written (which is the situation card 2 fixes), `find_duplicates` returns nothing either. After cards 1-4 landed, the running MCP server still holds the pre-fix DB (38,073 chunks, 0 with embeddings) because the indexer code change requires a fresh process to re-embed existing chunks. Running `code-context find duplicates` against the current DB would return 0 results — but for the same reason `search code` does, not a separate bug.

+- `query_ast.rs` — parses files at query time, doesn't read the DB index. Less risk; spot-check.
+- `get_callgraph.rs`, `get_blastradius.rs` — read `lsp_call_edges`. Same audit.

+### Suspected casualty: find_duplicates

-### Verification of `find_duplicates` against this workspace
+`swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/find_duplicates.rs:229` has `fn insert_chunk(conn, ..., embedding: &[f32])` — identical pattern to `search_code`. `find_duplicates` reads `ts_chunks.embedding`. If embeddings aren't being written (which is the situation card 2 fixes), `find_duplicates` returns nothing either. Verify after card 2 lands that `find_duplicates` works; if it doesn't, the missing wiring is somewhere else.

-Inconclusive on the live DB. After cards 1-4 landed, the running MCP server still holds the pre-fix DB (38,073 chunks, 0 with embeddings) because the indexer code change requires a fresh process to re-embed existing chunks. Running `code-context find duplicates` against the current DB would return 0 results — but for the same reason `search code` does, not a separate bug.
+### Files to audit

-`find_duplicates` is in the same situation as `search_code` was before cards 2+3: the indexer fix lands the data; the op consumes it. The right verification is the follow-up real-pipeline test (filed below).
+Walk every file in `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/`:
+- `search_code.rs` — known broken, fixed by cards 2+3, covered by card 4. Skip.
+- `find_duplicates.rs` — suspected broken (same pattern). Verify.
+- `search_symbol.rs` — reads `lsp_symbols`. Does the indexer populate it? Tests fixture-only?
+- `get_symbol.rs`, `get_definition.rs`, `get_hover.rs`, `get_references.rs`, `get_implementations.rs`, `get_inbound_calls.rs` — LSP-layered ops. Verify they have at least one test that exercises the real LSP indexer over a real temp file and gets a useful result.
+- `grep_code.rs` — reads `ts_chunks.text`. Probably fine since `text` IS written by the real indexer. Verify.
+- `list_symbol.rs`, `workspace_symbol_live.rs` — same audit.
+- `query_ast.rs` — parses files at query time, doesn't read the DB index. Less risk; spot-check.
+- `get_callgraph.rs`, `get_blastradius.rs` — read `lsp_call_edges`. Same audit.
+- `status.rs` — pure DB query against schema. Lower risk but verify it surfaces every layer's completeness (card 1 addresses this for embeddings).

-### Follow-up card filed
+### Approach

-- **01KRF4DHGBV4H00JE2NZFSMRV9** — End-to-end real-pipeline test coverage for code-context ops. Bundles e2e tests for the FIXTURE-ONLY ops by data layer: one for `find_duplicates` (embeddings), one for `grep_code` (chunk text), one for LSP-layered ops (`search_symbol`, `get_callgraph`, `get_blastradius`). Live-LSP-only ops are lower priority by design.
+1. For each op file, list its `#[test]` and `#[tokio::test]` functions.
+2. For each test, classify the data-setup mechanism:
+   - `test_db()` + raw SQL inserts → FIXTURE-ONLY
+   - calls `index_discovered_files_async` / `spawn_indexing_worker` / real LSP daemon → REAL-PIPELINE
+   - Mixed (some of each) → REAL-PIPELINE counts only if AT LEAST one test exercises the production indexer end-to-end
+3. Build a table in the task description: op | test-count | classification | risk.
+4. For each op classified FIXTURE-ONLY where the op is exposed in the MCP tool description, create a follow-up kanban card titled `End-to-end test for <op>` modeled on card 4 (`01KREM6B7X01T8WXDA258DS2K9`).
+5. After the audit, add a project memory entry (`/Users/wballard/.claude/projects/-Users-wballard-github-swissarmyhammer-swissarmyhammer/memory/`) documenting the pattern and the rule that fixture-only coverage doesn't count as feature coverage for advertised capabilities.

-### Project memory entry
+### Verification of find_duplicates as a worked example

-Added at `/Users/wballard/.claude/projects/-Users-wballard-github-swissarmyhammer-swissarmyhammer/memory/feedback_fixture_only_anti_pattern.md`. Indexed in `MEMORY.md`. Names the anti-pattern, the rule, and points at the canonical reference test (`swissarmyhammer-tools/tests/integration/semantic_search_e2e.rs`).
+After card 2 lands (embeddings are now written), manually run `code-context find duplicates --file-path <some-file>` against this workspace. Expected behavior: returns at least one duplicate group somewhere in this 65-crate monorepo. If it returns empty groups, file a follow-up card with the bug.
